name: Johnny Mercer
description: American lyricist, songwriter, singer and record executive (1909–1976)
abstract: John Herndon Mercer (November 18, 1909 – June 25, 1976) was an American lyricist, songwriter, and singer, as well as a record label executive who co-founded Capitol Records with music industry businessmen Buddy DeSylva and Glenn E. Wallichs.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Salute to Eddie Condon

works_created.t: I'm an Old Cowhand from the Rio Grande

about_quotes.speaker: Keith Ingham
about_quotes.evidence: Harold Arlen and Johnny Mercer. That was probably one of their biggest hits simply because that catchy opening phrase
about_quotes.doc_title: Keith Ingham interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Jonah Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I knew guys like Johnny Mercer and Bing, you know, come in the Onyx. Johnny used to sing with us.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Rowles
about_quotes.evidence: Because we did "Morning Star," and it was my tune with Johnny Mercer's lyrics

about_quotes.speaker: John Budd
about_quotes.evidence: Of the ones that I’ve met, the one that I am most fond of is Johnny Mercer. I met him by accident... I became friendly with a bunch at Eddie Condon’s place.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Budd interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997 [transcript only]

about_quotes.speaker: Leonidas Raymond Young
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] The President was Clyde Wallach then? Johnny Mercer? [INTERVIEWEE] No. It was the one that married one of the King Sisters.

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Bushkin
about_quotes.evidence: Abe Berman... represented Harold Arlen, Johnny Mercer, Vince Youmans... he sold Capitol Records for Johnny Mercer and Buddy DeSilva
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Bushkin interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999
